1: Woe to those who plan iniquity — hoy choshevei aven ve-po'alei ra': the hoy (woe) oracle targets premeditated sin — plotting evil on their beds at night and executing it at dawn. Ba-or ha-boqer ya'asuha ki yesh-le'el yaddam (at the dawn of morning they carry it out, because it is within their power) — yesh-le'el yaddam (it is in the power of their hands) — they have the social and economic power to execute what they planned; power enables injustice.
2: They covet fields and seize them — ve-chamdu sadot ve-gazalu: the accusation echoes the Decalogue's final prohibition (lo tachmod — you shall not covet, Exod 20:17; Deut 5:21) and its consequence in the Naboth narrative (1 Kgs 21). Ve-'ish u-veito nasau (and a man and his house) — the legal unit of a man's household (bayit). Nachalah (inheritance) is the covenant land grant: inalienable under Mosaic law (Lev 25:23-28; Num 36:7-9); seizing it violated both the Decalogue and the land-tenure laws. The latifundialization of the land (rich consolidating small farms) is the specific economic crime targeted.
3: I am devising disaster against this family — hineni choshev al-hamishpachah hazot ra'ah: the same verb (chashav = plan, devise) used against the wicked planners in v1 is now used of YHWH's counter-planning. The lex talionis is structural: they planned evil (choshevei aven); YHWH devises evil against them (choshev ra'ah). Lo-telkhu romah (you shall not walk haughtily) — the upright, proud posture (romah) of the powerful will become impossible under the yoke of judgment.
4: In that day a taunt shall be raised against you — ba-yom hahu yissa' 'aleykhem mashal: the taunt-song (mashal) inverts the property-seizure: chelq ammi yamir (the portion of my people is exchanged) — chelq (portion, allotment) is the covenant land share given by lot; yamir (is exchanged) — the fields they seized are redistributed to others. La-shoveiv sadoteinu yechalleq (to the faithless he apportions our fields) — the redistributor (shoveiv = turncoat, apostate, rebel) now holds the fields the covenant people once owned; the judgment mirrors the crime.
5: You shall have no one to cast the lot in the assembly of the LORD — lachen lo-yihyeh lekha mashliakh chevel be-goral: the goral (lot) was the mechanism for distributing covenant land in the original division under Joshua (Num 26:55-56; Josh 14-21). To have no one casting the lot in the qahal YHWH (assembly of the LORD) means exclusion from the covenant community's land-rights — they who seized others' lots lose their own participation in the covenant distribution system.
6: Stop preaching! — so they preach — al-tittfu (do not drip / do not prophesy) — nataf (to drip, to let words drip down) is used for prophetic speech (Amos 7:16; Ezek 21:2); the word-picture of prophecy as falling rain/dew. The opponents command silence: al-tittfu to the prophets. The irony: they are preaching (yattifun) the command not to preach — they themselves become preachers while silencing others.
7: Is the Spirit of the LORD cut short? — ha-qatsar ruach YHWH: the divine Spirit cannot be curtailed (qatsar = shortened, constrained). The rhetorical questions defend genuine prophecy: YHWH's Spirit is not cut short; his words do good to those who walk uprightly. Ha-elo de-varav yeitivu (do not my words do good to him who walks uprightly?) — the criterion: YHWH's genuine words bring good to the upright, not just to those who want to hear comfortable oracles.
8: My people have risen up as an enemy — ve-etmul ammi le-oyev yaqum: the people's behavior toward their own members constitutes treating them as enemies. Shalmat ta'deru (you strip the cloak from) — ta'der (strip off, peel away) applied to the salmah (outer garment/cloak) — stripping the cloak of those returning from war who want only peace (shuvi milchamah betach). The outer cloak was the poor person's only bedding; cf. Exod 22:26-27; Amos 2:8.
9: The women of my people you drive out from their pleasant houses — neshei ammi tegarshun mibet ta'anugeha: widows and women of forcibly seized estates are expelled from their homes. Me'al 'olaleha tiqchu hadari le'olam (from their young children you take away my blessing forever) — hadar (beauty, adornment, blessing) taken from young children permanently; the dispossession of the next generation from the covenant blessing.
10: Arise and depart, for this is not your place of rest — qumu lekhu ki lo-zo hamenuchah: menuchah (resting place, rest) is the covenant-land term (Deut 12:9; Ps 95:11; 132:14). The land as 'rest' for YHWH's people is being cancelled: be'ur tumme'ah (because of its uncleanness) — ritual-cultic defilement of the land through injustice and idolatry (cf. Lev 18:24-28; Num 35:33-34) removes its status as covenant menuchah.
11: If someone walks in a spirit of wind and deceit — lulei ish holekh ruach ve-shaqer kizzev: the false prophet is described by his medium — spirit (ruach) = wind, and deception (shaqer). Ettof lekha la-yayin ve-lash-shekhar (I will preach to you of wine and strong drink) — nataf (drip/preach) again; the false prophet promises material abundance (wine, beer). This is what the people want to hear — comfortable prosperity oracles — making such a prophet popular. The sarcasm is bitter.
12: I will surely gather all of you, O Jacob — asof e'esof Ya'aqov kulakh: the double infinitive absolute construction (asof e'esof) expresses absolute certainty of the gathering. Kaqqor be-dovrah ke-'eder be-tokh hadderotam (like sheep in a fold, like a flock in its pasture) — two shepherd images; dor (pasture enclosure) and derotam (their folds). The pastoral reversal of the exile's scattering. The noisy multitude (tehimena me-adam) signals restored community after judgment.
13: The one who breaks through goes up before them — alah ha-poretz lifneihem: poretz (the one who breaks through, breach-maker) is a striking messianic-shepherd image. Military language: breaking through enemy lines. Yafretsu va'avru sha'ar (they break out, pass through the gate) — YHWH's people burst through the city gate of exile. Malcham lifneihem ya'avor (their king goes before them) and ve-YHWH be-ro'sham (the LORD at their head) — the king and YHWH in tandem at the front of the procession; the new exodus imagery with YHWH as the divine warrior going ahead of his people.
